name: Dynamic Hockey Warm-up
purpose: Raise body temperature and prepare ankles, hips, shoulders, and skating posture.
setup: Create a clear 10-yard lane with a cone at each end.
setupChecklist: Place one cone at each end
Tie shoes and remove loose equipment
instructions: Jog down and back at an easy pace.
Complete lateral shuffles facing the same direction each way.
Perform walking lunges with an overhead reach.
Finish with two progressive accelerations at 60% and 80% speed.
progression: Add two short reaction accelerations after the movement series.
regression: Walk every movement and shorten the range of the lunges.
safetyNotes: Use a dry, clear surface and stop if any movement causes pain.

name: 10-Yard Sprint
purpose: Develop an explosive first three steps and strong acceleration posture.
setup: Mark a start line and finish line exactly 10 yards apart with safe slowdown space.
setupChecklist: Add at least 8 yards to slow down
instructions: Start in an athletic hockey stance behind the line.
Drive forward with a strong first step.
Accelerate through the finish line.
Walk back and rest fully before the next rep.
commonMistakes: Reaching too far with the foot
progression: React to a visual or voice start signal.
safetyNotes: Use a flat non-slip surface with enough room to slow down.

name: 5-10-5 Agility Shuttle
purpose: Build efficient direction changes, braking strength, and re-acceleration.
setup: Place three cones in a straight line, five yards apart.
setupChecklist: Check the turning area for grip
instructions: Start at the middle cone in a low stance.
Sprint five yards and touch beyond the outside cone.
Turn and sprint ten yards to the far cone.
Touch beyond the line, turn, and finish through the middle.
progression: React to a random first-direction call.
regression: Practice each change of direction at half speed.
safetyNotes: Wear shoes with grip and avoid wet or uneven surfaces.

name: Bodyweight Split Squat
purpose: Build single-leg strength and control for skating stride positions.
setup: Stand in a staggered stance beside a wall or sturdy support if needed.
setupChecklist: Set feet on two train tracks
instructions: Lower the back knee toward the floor under control.
Keep the front foot flat and knee tracking over toes.
Pause briefly at the bottom.
Drive through the front foot to stand.
commonMistakes: Pushing only from the back leg
progression: Hold a light dumbbell or add a two-second pause.
regression: Reduce depth and lightly hold a wall.
safetyNotes: Use a pain-free range and stop if knee discomfort increases.

name: Lateral Bound and Stick
purpose: Develop lateral power and controlled single-leg landings.
setup: Use a clear flat area with two markers about three feet apart.
instructions: Load one leg with hips back.
Push sideways and land softly on the opposite leg.
Hold the landing for two seconds.
Reset before bounding back.
progression: Increase distance slightly while maintaining a stable landing.
regression: Use a lateral step and balance hold instead of a jump.
safetyNotes: Only increase distance when every landing is controlled.

name: Head-Up Stickhandling
purpose: Control the puck with soft hands while scanning and processing callouts.
setup: Create a four-cone box and place a parent or callout cards in front.
setupChecklist: Place four cones in a box
instructions: Begin in an athletic hockey stance.
Move the puck around the outside and through the middle.
Lift the eyes for every callout.
Keep handling while answering correctly.
progression: Use faster callouts, a smaller space, or a puck instead of a ball.
regression: Use a ball, slow the pace, and make fewer callouts.
safetyNotes: Keep people outside stick range and remove loose pucks.

name: 50-Shot Accuracy
purpose: Improve repeatable shooting mechanics and four-corner accuracy.
setup: Secure a net or shooting tarp and organize 50 pucks in five groups of ten.
setupChecklist: Confirm area behind target is safe
Set 50 pucks in groups of ten
instructions: Take ten controlled shots per set.
Alternate or call the target before each shot.
Reset balance and hand position.
Count accurate target hits.
commonMistakes: Looking at the puck through release
progression: Shoot after a lateral pull or quick catch-and-release.
regression: Move closer and use wrist shots only.
safetyNotes: Only shoot into a secured target with nobody behind or beside the net.

name: Puck-Control Weave
purpose: Develop puck control through direction changes while maintaining speed.
setup: Place six cones in a straight line with enough space to weave safely.
instructions: Start under control before the first cone.
Move the puck around each cone while the feet follow.
Keep the puck within comfortable reach.
Accelerate through the final cone and stop safely.
commonMistakes: Reaching too far for the puck
progression: Reduce cone spacing or add a head-up callout.
regression: Increase cone spacing and use a ball.
safetyNotes: Keep the course clear and leave room to slow down.
